text: Upon diagnosis with cancer, determining the most suitable treatment for their condition is a very difficult and complex decision for a cancer patient. The goal of this chapter is to present an interdisciplinary research project, which aims to computerize the production of individualized treatment summaries that can support shared decision making between a patient and physician. To this end, we linked two large sources from the Netherlands Comprehensive Cancer Organisation (IKNL) – the Netherlands Cancer Registry and the PROFILES dataset – and used information on individualized treatment per cancer site and patient characteristic to create insight in cancer treatment for patients with a particular cancer type and profile. To create the actual summaries that support SDM, we converted the data into more human readable format including both prose explanations as well as narrative orientations. We reflect on the applicability and the limitations of the research approach we adopted, and we hypothesize how the work can be extended towards other health areas, and that it reflects on the underlying value of the research project.
